reasoning: Each informal hypothesis has a direct Lean counterpart: (a) continuity on x ≥ 0 ↔ ContinuousOn f (Set.Ici 0); (b) f' exists on x > 0 ↔ DifferentiableOn ℝ f (Set.Ioi 0); (c) f(0)=0 ↔ f 0 = 0; (d) f' monotonically increasing ↔ MonotoneOn (deriv f) (Set.Ioi 0). Because hf2 asserts differentiability on (0, ∞), `deriv f` on that set agrees with the classical derivative, so hypothesis (d) is correctly captured. The conclusion `MonotoneOn (fun x => f x / x) (Set.Ioi 0)` matches "g(x) = f(x)/x is monotonically increasing for x > 0", and Rudin's "monotonically increasing" corresponds to non-decreasing (Monotone), not strict. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
